# Regulatory Document Translation & Localization — L1 Capability Map

**Domain:** Regulatory Document Translation & Localization
**Industry:** Med-Tech (Abbott Enterprise)
**Lens:** Business + IT (Both)
**Date:** 2026-03-16
**Generated by:** Capability Mapper v1.3

> **Scope:** Full translation & localization domain — IFUs, labels, regulatory submissions, market-specific localization.
> Regulatory Affairs is a standalone primary domain at Abbott; this map covers the translation capability layer that serves it.
> Abbott boundaries applied: Privacy standalone (excluded), OEC standalone (excluded), Regulatory Affairs standalone (this map serves RA, not replaces it).

---

## MAP A — Business Capability View

### Primary Activities
*Capabilities that directly produce translated regulatory content for market release*

> ⚡ High/Med/Low = digital intensity · 🎯 Edge = differentiating, Core = essential, Commodity = table stakes · 👁 Business/IT/Dual = ownership

**1.** 📋 **Regulatory Document Translation**
Translates IFUs, labels, CERs, SSCPs, and regulatory submissions into required languages for each market, meeting jurisdictional accuracy requirements.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

**2.** 🏷️ **Label Management & Lifecycle**
Creates, versions, and manages market-specific product labels and packaging content across languages from initial design through post-market updates.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

**3.** ✅ **Linguistic Validation**
Ensures patient-facing and clinical materials meet regulatory accuracy and comprehension standards per FDA 21 CFR, EU MDR Article 10, and market-specific requirements.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

**4.** 🌍 **Market Submission Packaging**
Assembles and submits translated regulatory documents to market-specific health authorities as part of product registration and approval processes.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

**5.** 🔄 **Post-Market Translation Maintenance**
Updates and re-validates translations when source documents change due to product revisions, regulatory changes, or safety field corrective actions.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

### Support Activities
*Capabilities that govern, enable, and sustain translation quality and compliance*

**6.** 📐 **Translation Governance & Language Policy**
Defines required languages per market, sets quality and compliance standards, and governs the translation function across global regulatory programs.
⚡ Low · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

**7.** 📖 **Regulatory Terminology Management**
Builds and maintains standardized glossaries and term bases ensuring consistency and accuracy across all translated regulatory content and submissions.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

**8.** 🤝 **LSP & Vendor Management**
Selects, manages, and evaluates Language Service Providers against regulatory quality standards, compliance requirements, cost, and delivery performance.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

**9.** 🔍 **Translation Quality Assurance**
Reviews, audits, and approves translations for regulatory accuracy, consistency, and compliance before market submission or product release.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

**10.** 📅 **Market Release Coordination**
Aligns translation completion timelines with global market release schedules, coordinating across regulatory affairs, supply chain, and country affiliates.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 Business

---

## MAP B — IT Capability View

### Primary Activities
*Technology capabilities that directly enable translation delivery and regulatory submission*

> ⚡ High/Med/Low = digital intensity · 🎯 Edge = differentiating, Core = essential, Commodity = table stakes · 👁 Business/IT/Dual = ownership

**1.** ⚙️ **Translation Management System (TMS)**
Core platform managing translation project intake, workflow routing, translator assignments, progress tracking, and delivery across all regulated content types.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**2.** 🏷️ **Label Management System**
Platform for authoring, versioning, and distributing market-specific product labels and IFUs in approved languages, integrated with print and submission workflows.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**3.** 🤖 **AI-Assisted Translation & MT Engine**
Machine translation with human review workflow for regulated content — accelerates translation velocity while maintaining quality thresholds required for regulatory submission.
⚡ High · 🎯 Edge · 👁 Dual

**4.** 🔗 **Regulatory Submission Platform Integration**
Integration layer connecting translation outputs to regulatory submission systems (eCTD, Veeva Vault) for compliant market registration packaging and delivery.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**5.** 🔔 **Change Detection & Update Automation**
Automated detection of source document changes (product updates, safety corrections, label revisions) triggering translation update and re-validation workflows.
⚡ High · 🎯 Edge · 👁 IT

### Support Activities
*Technology capabilities that sustain the translation platform ecosystem*

**6.** 🧠 **Translation Memory & Terminology Platform**
Centralized repository of approved translations, term bases, and glossaries enabling consistency, reuse, and cost reduction across projects and languages.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**7.** 📊 **Translation Analytics & Cycle Time Reporting**
Dashboards tracking translation progress, cycle times, quality error rates, LSP performance, and market release readiness across all active projects.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 Dual

**8.** 🌐 **LSP Portal & Vendor Integration**
Secure external-facing platform enabling Language Service Providers to receive source documents, submit translations, and participate in review workflows with full audit trail.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**9.** 🔐 **Document Security & Audit Infrastructure**
Access controls, version management, and audit trail infrastructure for all regulated translation content — ensuring traceability for regulatory inspections and submissions.
⚡ High · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

**10.** 🔄 **PLM / Source Document Integration**
Integration with Product Lifecycle Management and document management systems to receive authoritative source document updates and trigger downstream translation workflows.
⚡ Med · 🎯 Core · 👁 IT

---

📌 Org Context Note

This map reflects Med-Tech enterprise defaults for a global regulatory translation function.
Boundaries assumed: Translation & Localization is a capability layer serving the standalone Regulatory Affairs domain; Abbott BTS owns and operates the IT platforms; LSPs are external vendors managed through a vendor management capability.
Adjust if: Translation is fully outsourced end-to-end — several IT capabilities collapse to vendor oversight only.
Adjust if: Label Management is owned by a separate Labeling & Artwork function — split Label Management & Lifecycle out of this map and note the dependency.

---

🔎 Completeness Check

Wide scan zones covered (IT lens — all 8):
- Platform & Tooling: ✓ TMS, Label Management System, MT Engine
- Data & Analytics: ✓ Translation Analytics & Cycle Time Reporting
- Knowledge Management: ✓ Translation Memory & Terminology Platform
- Workflow & Process Automation: ✓ Change Detection & Update Automation, AI-Assisted Translation
- Integration & API Management: ✓ Regulatory Submission Integration, PLM/Source Document Integration
- Infrastructure & Security: ✓ Document Security & Audit Infrastructure
- Vendor & Spend Management: ✓ LSP Portal & Vendor Integration
- User Experience & Adoption: ⚠️ No standalone L1 — UX needs covered within TMS and LSP Portal. Flag if internal requester experience is a known pain point — could warrant its own capability.

Capabilities I considered but excluded:
- Translation Cost Management — excluded; absorbed into LSP & Vendor Management (Business) and Analytics (IT). Elevate to L1 if translation spend is a strategic focus area.
- Country Affiliate Portal — excluded; covered within LSP Portal scope. Elevate if affiliates have distinct access and workflow needs from external LSPs.
- Regulatory Intelligence for Language Requirements — excluded; owned by Regulatory Affairs, not this domain.

Domain watchlist flags triggered:
- Regulatory Translations not in domain watchlist — general completeness reasoning applied. Recommend adding this domain to the watchlist if it will be mapped frequently.

Recommendation: Validate whether AI-Assisted Translation is live or aspirational at Abbott — if aspirational, downgrade from Competitive Edge to Core Capability target and flag as a GenAI opportunity.
